refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity output of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: operator capability (on-site vs. remote), task execution, measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety plan requirements were met during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency sufficient to achieve target throughput.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, effective remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety plan compliance and operational policies in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect, aggregate, and report productivity telemetry (volume, time, incidents) for KPI evaluation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with remote teleoperation and real-time safety monitoring, collecting productivity metrics via onboard sensors and reporting via network link.
DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable attachments, teleoperation with dual-operator redundancy, and safety services with predictive risk assessment.
DP-C: Implement a vehicle with autonomous task planning capability, operator-in-the-loop teleoperation for override, and safety services with geofencing and collision avoidance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with remote teleoperation and real-time safety monitoring, collecting productivity metrics via onboard sensors and reporting via network link.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical efficiency and teleoperation latency are coupled: poor network link quality degrades operator control responsiveness, reducing effective productivity.
Safety Services and Vehicle control are coupled: safety constraints (e.g., speed limits, geofencing) directly impact task cycle time and throughput.
Telemetry collection and Teleoperation are coupled: both require continuous network link; link failure affects both control and KPI reporting.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are expected and manageable via interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). DP-A avoids unnecessary complexity (DP-B's redundancy, DP-C's autonomy) while meeting the core requirement. Recommend explicit latency and availability SLAs for Network Link to mitigate coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target with teleoperation; risk: inadequate operator training could mask vehicle capability shortfalls.
Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil type, terrain, weather) are representative of trial environment; risk: productivity_target may not generalize to different conditions.
Assumption: Network link reliability is ≥ 99% availability during operations; risk: link outages directly halt productivity and safety monitoring.
Assumption: Onboard sensors (volume, time, incident logging) are calibrated and trustworthy; risk: measurement error could invalidate KPI evaluation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) Network Link SLA (latency ≤ X ms, availability ≥ Y%), (2) Sensor calibration and validation protocol, (3) Operator training and certification requirements, (4) Safety constraint impact on cycle time (quantified as % throughput reduction).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration boundary encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and their interconnections via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. Responsible for end-to-end productivity KPI delivery and compliance with worksite safety plan.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Productivity metrics (volume moved, time elapsed, task completion status, incident logs) reported from Vehicle and Safety Services to external monitoring/evaluation system.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task instructions (dig location, load target, dump zone) received from Site Manager or Tele-operator and dispatched to Vehicle control.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Real-time safety alerts (geofence breach, collision risk, operator override, policy violation) issued by Safety Services to Site Manager and Tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication backbone for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and operator control commands between Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and remote monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are contained within this module. Minimal diffusion: only external stakeholder interactions (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager) cross the boundary.

Vehicle contains mechanical/hydraulic task execution and onboard sensors. However, productivity KPI requires integration with Teleoperation (operator control), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and Telemetry (reporting). Significant diffusion: Vehicle alone cannot achieve productivity_target without external control and safety coordination.

Teleoperation provides operator interface and control commands. Productivity depends on Vehicle responsiveness, Safety Services constraints, and Network Link quality. High diffusion: Teleoperation is a critical enabler but not self-contained; requires tight coupling with Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services monitors and enforces safety policies, issuing SafetyAlert and constraints to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Moderately contained: safety logic is localized, but enforcement requires Vehicle and Teleoperation compliance. Diffusion: safety constraints directly impact productivity (cycle time reduction).

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (system integrator); with sub-ownership delegated to Vehicle (mechanical/hydraulic), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (safety policy enforcement).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Productivity KPI (FR-NGHE-001) is best owned at the NGHE Solution level because it is an emergent property requiring coordinated interaction of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. No single sub-module can independently achieve the KPI; the system integrator must orchestrate interfaces and validate end-to-end performance.
containment_risks: Risk: Vehicle mechanical efficiency is a prerequisite but not sufficient; if Vehicle underperforms, Teleoperation and Safety Services cannot compensate, leading to KPI failure. Mitigation: establish Vehicle performance baseline (e.g., cycle time, fuel efficiency) before system integration.

Risk: Teleoperation latency or operator skill gaps could degrade productivity even if Vehicle is capable. Mitigation: define operator training and certification requirements; validate teleoperation latency SLA.

Risk: Safety Services constraints (speed limits, geofencing, collision avoidance) may reduce effective throughput below productivity_target. Mitigation: quantify safety constraint impact on cycle time; validate that constrained throughput still meets target.

Risk: Network Link failures halt both control and telemetry, preventing productivity measurement and safety monitoring. Mitigation: establish Network Link SLA (availability, latency); implement fallback communication or safe shutdown procedures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign ownership of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution (system integrator). Decompose into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Establish interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with explicit SLAs. Define integration test plan to validate end-to-end productivity KPI.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle + teleoperation + safety services + telemetry) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity_target). The DP specifies a coherent solution concept: operator (on-site or remote) controls vehicle via teleoperation, safety services enforce constraints, and onboard sensors report productivity metrics. No logical gaps or contradictions. Couplings are identified and manageable via interface contracts.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement logic may be split between Vehicle (sensor collection), Telemetry (aggregation/reporting), and NGHE Solution (KPI calculation). Mitigation: centralize KPI calculation in a single module (e.g., NGHE Solution or dedicated telemetry service); define clear data ownership.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety constraint enforcement is distributed across Safety Services (policy logic), Vehicle (mechanical limits), and Teleoperation (operator override). Mitigation: establish a safety arbitration interface; Safety Services has final authority over constraint enforcement.

Diffusion Risk 3: Network Link is a shared dependency for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and operator control. Link failure cascades across all functions. Mitigation: implement redundant communication paths or graceful degradation (e.g., safe shutdown if link lost).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for FR-NGHE-001 because it encompasses all sub-modules and interfaces required to achieve the KPI. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contained within NGHE Solution and coordinate via well-defined interfaces. No module is duplicated or orphaned. Ownership is clear: NGHE Solution integrator is accountable for end-to-end KPI delivery.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial: measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period, validate safety compliance, and compare against productivity_target. Telemetry interface provides data for KPI calculation. Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces enable controlled test scenarios. Network Link must be operational and meet SLA for trial to be valid.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution (system integrator). Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and measurable acceptance criteria. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) define how sub-modules coordinate to achieve the top-level KPI.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Explicitly define productivity_target value (e.g., 50 m³/h) and evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour week) in FR-NGHE-001. Current requirement is parameterized; concrete values are needed for acceptance testing.
Revision 2: Specify Network Link SLA (latency, availability, bandwidth) in DP-A. Teleoperation and telemetry depend critically on link quality; SLA must be quantified and validated.

Revision 3: Define safety constraint impact on productivity in DP-A. Quantify how geofencing, speed limits, and collision avoidance reduce effective throughput. Validate that constrained throughput still meets productivity_target.
Revision 4: Establish operator training and certification requirements in DP-A. Operator skill is a critical variable; training must be standardized and validated before KPI evaluation.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that is not atomic. It requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle task execution, teleoperation control, safety services, telemetry reporting) to enable detailed design and verification. DP-A is sound and addresses the requirement, but implementation requires further refinement of sub-DPs and sub-IMs. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be decomposed further to assign ownership and define detailed interfaces. Proceed to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency sufficient to achieve target throughput (e.g., cycle time ≤ X seconds per task).
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, effective remote task execution (e.g., latency ≤ Y ms, video frame rate ≥ Z fps).

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety plan compliance and operational policies in real-time (e.g., geofence enforcement, collision avoidance, policy violation alerts).

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect, aggregate, and report productivity telemetry (volume, time, incidents) for KPI evaluation (e.g., telemetry update frequency ≥ W Hz, data accuracy ≥ V%).

revise.why: FR-NGHE-001 requires parameterization: define concrete productivity_target value, evaluation period, and safety compliance criteria. Current requirement is too abstract for detailed design and verification. Revised FR should specify measurable acceptance criteria (e.g., 'achieve ≥ 50 m³/h over 40-hour evaluation week with zero safety incidents').

assumptions: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target with teleoperation.
Worksite conditions (soil type, terrain, weather) are representative of trial environment.
Network link reliability is ≥ 99% availability during operations.
Onboard sensors (volume, time, incident logging) are calibrated and trustworthy.
Safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing) are compatible with productivity_target.
Evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour week) is sufficient to establish statistically valid productivity baseline.
open_risks: Risk: Network Link failure cascades across Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and operator control. Mitigation: establish SLA and redundancy strategy.
Risk: Safety constraint impact on productivity is unknown. Mitigation: quantify constraint impact (e.g., % throughput reduction) and validate against productivity_target.
Risk: Operator skill variability could mask vehicle capability shortfalls or inflate productivity claims. Mitigation: standardize operator training and certification.
Risk: Sensor calibration and data quality are not validated. Mitigation: establish sensor validation protocol and data quality assurance process.

Risk: Worksite conditions may differ from trial environment, affecting generalizability of productivity_target. Mitigation: conduct trials in representative conditions; document assumptions about soil, terrain, weather.
evidence_needed: Vehicle mechanical efficiency baseline (cycle time, fuel consumption, attachment performance).
Teleoperation latency and operator situational awareness validation (e.g., user study, latency measurement).
Safety Services constraint impact quantification (e.g., simulation or trial data showing throughput reduction).
Network Link SLA validation (e.g., latency, availability, bandwidth measurements during trial).
Sensor calibration and data quality validation (e.g., volume measurement accuracy, time logging precision).
Operator training and certification documentation (e.g., training curriculum, certification test results).
Representative operations trial data (e.g., productivity measurements, safety incident logs, telemetry records).
